response.justification: Bogdan is a highly skilled Staff/Senior engineer with 15+ years of experience spanning fullstack, embedded, mobile, and AI tooling — technically well above the Junior/Mid-Level bar on every dimension. The concern is not competence but fit: this role explicitly targets entry-level candidates, and hiring someone at Bogdan's seniority creates real retention risk and likely a compensation mismatch. His TypeScript, Node.js, REST, and clean-architecture credentials are excellent, and his current AI/MCP work at Naver adds rare bonus value. A conversation is warranted only to understand why he is applying for a junior role, as the answer could either disqualify or reframe the opportunity.
strengths: Deep TypeScript and Node.js expertise across multiple production environments, directly matching the core stack requirement
Demonstrated commitment to software craftsmanship — TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD — aligning with the role's clean architecture interest
Current hands-on experience with LLMs and AI-agent infrastructure (Naver, MCP frameworks) covers the highest-value nice-to-have
concerns: Significantly overqualified: Staff/Senior-level with 15+ years; this is a junior/mid role and the seniority mismatch creates high attrition risk
PostgreSQL not explicitly mentioned; listed skills show MySQL/SQL and MongoDB, leaving relational-DB depth in the preferred technology unverified
React exposure is surface-level in the resume (one mention at Tencent); depth in the primary frontend framework for this role is unclear
